name: Canonical Edge Types
overview: TrustDB defines 11 canonical edge types that cover the full spectrum of entity relationships: structural, semantic, temporal, spatial, governance, and epistemic. Edges are first-class storage primitives with their own confidence envelopes, provenance chains, and CBAC governance. Edge creation runs at 47,600 edges/sec and typed filter queries at 176µs.

howItWorks.title: Edge Type System
howItWorks.description: 11 canonical types: CONTAINS, REFERENCES, DERIVED_FROM, TEMPORAL_SEQUENCE, SPATIAL_PROXIMITY, CLASSIFIES, GOVERNS, CONFLICTS_WITH, SUPPORTS, SUPERSEDES, CO_OCCURS. Each type has defined semantics, directionality, and cardinality constraints.

howItWorks.title: Storage as Primitives
howItWorks.description: Edges are stored in the B-epsilon tree as EdgeEntry records, keyed by (source_id, edge_type, target_id). This enables efficient prefix scans for 'all edges of type X from entity Y' without a separate graph index.

howItWorks.title: Confidence & Governance
howItWorks.description: Each edge carries its own ConfidenceEnvelope (edge confidence can differ from node confidence) and inherits governance from both endpoints via REWG bitwise intersection.

howItWorks.title: Typed Filter Query
howItWorks.description: Querying edges by type uses the B-epsilon tree's prefix scan on (source_id, edge_type). The 176µs filter time includes CBAC clearance checks on both the edge and target entity.

comparisons.source: Purpose-built graph DB; faster for pure graph writes
Requires JOIN on edge table + type filter

comparisons.narrative: Neo4j and DGraph are faster at pure graph operations — they are purpose-built graph databases. TrustDB's graph capability is deliberately integrated into a multi-modal storage engine rather than being a standalone graph DB. The advantage is that graph edges share the same MVCC snapshot, confidence model, and governance layer as all other data. The tradeoff is lower raw graph throughput compared to dedicated graph databases.

competitors.approach: Native graph storage with relationship types and properties
competitors.limitation: Faster for pure graph ops; but separate system from document/search/spatial stores; no confidence envelopes on relationships
Higher throughput at scale; but separate service; no governance-integrated edges
Cypher support but relational storage underneath; JOIN-based traversal slower than native graph
Separate managed service; no confidence or governance integration; per-query pricing
